skill.name: risk-metrics-calculation
skill.description: Calculate portfolio risk metrics including VaR, CVaR, Sharpe, Sortino, and drawdown analysis. Use when measuring portfolio risk, implementing risk limits, or building risk monitoring systems.
skill.summary: Calculate portfolio risk, tail loss, drawdown, stress, and risk-adjusted return metrics.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, external links, a network reconnaissance pattern, and a weak crypto pattern. Review found no executable shell calls, no active network code, no cryptographic implementation, and no prompt injection attempt. The remaining risk is low because the skill contains financial code examples that users should validate before production use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are Markdown code fences or inline file references. No shell command, Ruby backtick execution, or user-controlled command execution was found at these lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines show Markdown delimiters and inline file paths, not executable code. No command invocation or interpolation path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Links Flagged as Network Activity
low_findings.description: The flagged URLs are bibliography and documentation links in the Resources section. They are not fetched by code and do not transmit local data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are static Markdown links to books and documentation. There is no HTTP client code, credential handling, or data exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Correlation Guidance Flagged as Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged line is a risk-management warning about stress-period correlations. It is finance guidance, not scanning, probing, or network discovery logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line discusses portfolio correlation risk in plain text. No network API, host enumeration, or reconnaissance command appears near the finding.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Pattern in Skill Description
low_findings.description: The flagged description lists financial metrics such as Sharpe and Sortino. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is natural-language metadata for portfolio risk metrics. It contains no cryptographic function call or security-sensitive algorithm choice.

content.value_statement: Portfolio teams need consistent risk measures across returns, drawdowns, and stress scenarios. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical risk metric design and validation.
actual_capabilities: Explains VaR, CVaR, volatility, beta, drawdown, and risk-adjusted return calculations.
Provides implementation patterns for single-asset and portfolio-level risk analysis.
Guides rolling risk metrics for changing market regimes and monitoring windows.
Outlines historical, hypothetical, and Monte Carlo stress testing methods.
Includes best practices for assumptions, lookbacks, tail risk, and metric interpretation.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not audited production risk software.
It requires clean return data, benchmark data, and portfolio weights from the user.
It does not fetch market data or connect to broker, custodian, or risk systems.
It does not replace regulated financial, investment, or model risk review.

use_cases.title: Build a Daily Risk Report
use_cases.description: Create a repeatable report with volatility, VaR, CVaR, drawdown, and risk-adjusted return metrics.

use_cases.title: Implement Risk Limits
use_cases.description: Translate portfolio return data into metrics that support risk thresholds and escalation rules.

use_cases.title: Design Stress Tests
use_cases.description: Compare portfolio behavior across historical crises, custom shocks, and simulated loss scenarios.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Core Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose risk metrics for a daily portfolio report. Explain which inputs I need and how each metric should be interpreted.

prompt_templates.title: Calculate Tail Risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a VaR and CVaR calculation workflow for my return series. Include confidence levels, lookback choices, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Add Rolling Monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a rolling risk monitoring plan for volatility, Sharpe ratio, beta, and drawdown. Include window choices and alert thresholds.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Risk Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my portfolio risk methodology for model risk. Check assumptions, data quality, stress testing, and production monitoring gaps.

output_examples.input: I have daily returns for a multi-asset portfolio and need a risk dashboard.
output_examples.output: A dashboard plan with daily volatility, VaR, CVaR, max drawdown, Sharpe ratio, Sortino ratio, and benchmark-relative metrics.

output_examples.input: I need to set loss limits for a strategy with fat-tailed returns.
output_examples.output: A risk limit framework using historical VaR, CVaR, stress losses, drawdown duration, and documented escalation thresholds.

output_examples.input: I want to test my portfolio against crisis periods.
output_examples.output: A stress testing outline covering historical crisis windows, custom asset shocks, simulated paths, and limitations of each method.

best_practices: Use multiple metrics because volatility, tail loss, and drawdown capture different risks.
Document lookback windows, confidence levels, annualization choices, and data cleaning rules.
Validate results against known periods, benchmarks, and independent calculations before production use.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on VaR alone for portfolios with skewed or fat-tailed returns.
Do not mix daily, monthly, and annual inputs without explicit annualization rules.
Do not use short lookbacks as the only basis for risk limits.

faq.question: Does this skill calculate metrics directly from my files?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and implementation patterns. You must provide or connect the return data in your own environment.

faq.question: Can it help with VaR and CVaR?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers historical, parametric, and adjusted VaR concepts, plus CVaR for tail loss analysis.

faq.question: Does it include stress testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes historical crisis tests, hypothetical shocks, and Monte Carlo stress testing methods.

faq.question: Is this suitable for regulated reporting?
faq.answer: It can support methodology design, but regulated reporting needs independent validation and compliance review.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What data quality checks are important?
faq.answer: Check missing dates, stale prices, corporate actions, benchmark alignment, outliers, and consistent return frequency.
